subtopic: Carbon Credit Trading and Green Credit Program
title: Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS) and GHG Emissions
focus: Mechanisms of the CCTS, India's emission statistics from BUR-4, and other market mechanisms like PAT and REC.
rationale: This section covers the regulatory framework for carbon trading in India, including the roles of BEE and CERC, alongside crucial emissions data from BUR-4, making it highly testable for UPSC prelims.
sectionExcerpt: The Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS) is notified under the Energy Conservation Amendment Act, 2022. It operates as an intensity-based baseline-and-credit system covering Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions. The Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) is the administrator, and the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) is the regulator. India's 4th Biennial Update Report (BUR-4) notes total GHG emissions at 2,959 million tonnes of CO2 (excluding LULUCF), with the Energy sector contributing the highest (75.66%). Other market mechanisms include the PAT Scheme and Renewable Energy Certificates (REC).
evidenceBullets: CCTS is notified under the Energy Conservation Amendment Act (ECA), 2022.
BEE is the Administrator and CERC is the Regulator of Carbon Trading.
CCTS covers direct emissions (Scope 1) and indirect emissions from electricity/heat (Scope 2).
BUR-4 states Energy is the highest emitting sector (75.66%), followed by Agriculture (13.72%).
Carbon dioxide accounts for 80.53% of emissions, followed by Methane (13.32%).
PAT Scheme reduces Specific Energy Consumption in energy-intensive industries via ESCerts.

subtopic: Carbon Credit Trading and Green Credit Program
title: Green Credit Program (GCP) and Methodology
focus: Objectives, administration, and calculation methodology of the Green Credit Program under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
rationale: The Green Credit Program is a novel domestic initiative linked to Mission LiFE. Its specific administrative bodies (ICFRE), statutory backing (EPA 1986), and revised calculation methodologies are prime targets for objective questions.
sectionExcerpt: The Green Credit Program is an innovative market-based mechanism established under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, to incentivize voluntary environmental actions like afforestation and water conservation. It supports the Mission LiFE initiative. The Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE) is the administering body. Under the revised methodology for tree plantation, 1 Green Credit equals 1 new tree (over 5 years old) on degraded forest land with a minimum 40% canopy density. These specific credits are non-tradable and non-transferable. Eligible land parcels must be at least five hectares.
evidenceBullets: Established under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, with MoEFCC as the nodal agency.
Administered by the Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE), Dehradun.
Supports the Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) initiative.
Revised tree plantation methodology requires 5 years of restoration and a minimum 40% canopy density to claim credits.
1 Green Credit equals 1 new tree (over 5 years old), and these specific credits are non-tradable and non-transferable.
Identified land parcels for the program must be at least five hectares in size.

subtopic: Carbon Credit Trading and Green Credit Program
title: Article 6 of Paris Climate Agreement
focus: National Designated Authority (NDA) announcement for Article 6 implementation.
rationale: Although brief, the establishment of an NDA under the EPA 1986 for Article 6 of the Paris Agreement is a distinct and important regulatory update.
sectionExcerpt: The Ministry of Environment, Forests, and Climate Change (MoEFCC) announced the National Designated Authority (NDA) for the implementation of Article 6 of the Paris Agreement (2015) under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
evidenceBullets: MoEFCC announced the National Designated Authority (NDA) for implementing Article 6 of the Paris Agreement.
The implementation is notified under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
